_comment: Machine source of truth for the harness. Human-readable prose lives in the SYNAPSE→H22 checklist HTML; this file is what run.ts executes. Keep the two in sync (that sync IS task 0.3). 'verify' lists the deterministic checks from harness/verify/checks.py that gate the task. 'human_gate' marks a decision the harness must NOT make. 'mode' A runs now on H21; mode B is staged until harness/state/drop.json exists. v2: grafted with SYNAPSE_H22_BOUNDARY.md — D-H22-1/2/4 are now first-class (and critical), and the boundary non-goals are enforced as cross-cutting guardrails.

guardrails._comment: Run on EVERY sprint by checks.py, in addition to the task's own verify list. A guardrail with ok:false ⇒ deterministic FAIL (run.ts short-circuits before the Evaluator and writes a repair ticket). A guardrail with ok:null ⇒ not yet wired ⇒ WARN, never blocks. These enforce SYNAPSE_H22_BOUNDARY.md's non-goals so no Generator round can silently erode the moat: no APEX corpus in scout, no rigging-authoring drift, no mutation that bypasses the ledger, and no newly-introduced phantom hou.* API (dir()-symbol-table-gated). PLUS the green ratchet (suite_baseline): the full `pytest tests/` result must not regress vs the committed floor in harness/verify/suite_baseline.json — failures only DOWN, passes only UP — so no Generator round can green its target while reddening other tests.

tasks.title: Split the cp311 vendored seam (sidecar vs abi3)

human_gate.why: Bounded, evidence-backed direction — not a survival cliff. MEASURED: sidecar IPC (harness/notes/gate01_ipc_latency.py) runs p50 ~0.1-0.3ms — ~4 orders under the LLM + ~2s Houdini-cook floor, so IPC does NOT discriminate the arms. cp312/cp313 wheels are pre-cached at harness/state/wheel_cache/ (runbook docs/studio/UPGRADE.md Step 2a), so re-vendor-on-drop is cheap; if H22 ships cp311 this is a no-op. Sidecar is the durable fix, deferrable to the first post-release cycle. Harness verifies the property (brain_answers), not the mechanism; you commit the direction.

tasks.title: Build the H22 probe harness
tasks.note: On H21 the delta vs the H21-pinned constants should be ~empty — that empty diff is the proof the probe works before the drop makes the diff real.

tasks.title: Single-source the version
Fold the duplicate ui/ tree into panel/
Pre-stage the demo scaffold (.hip, shot, OCIO)
Lock the demo runsheet
tasks.note: Content + rehearsal. Harness confirms presence/beat-count; the rehearsal is yours.

tasks.title: Rehearse the clean-machine install on H21
Scaffold the external-MCP provider seam (truth-contract envelope) against a mock APEX MCP
tasks.note: D-H22-1 scaffold. H22's MCP does not exist yet — build + contract-test the provider abstraction against a mock so drop day is wiring-in, not design. The mock's tool list stands in for the real surface; task 1.7 swaps it for the shipped build. Normalize on the Anthropic-shaped envelope, NOT a gateway.

tasks.title: Wire scout to federate the (mock) APEX MCP — consume, don't rebuild
tasks.note: D-H22-2 scaffold. APEX syntax comes ONLY from the federated provider. exists_in_runtime stays authoritative. Scout must own NO APEX-syntax corpus — that is the standing guardrail.

tasks.title: Install H22 clean + Synapse via package

human_gate.why: Can't install software that doesn't exist yet.

tasks.title: Capture the three numbers: Python · USD · PySide

human_gate.why: A human reads these first. Writing drop.json is the Mode-B trigger.

tasks.title: Verify the cp311 fix holds on H22's Python
Fire the probe → API delta report
synapse_doctor green on H22
Panel reads correctly in H22's new theme
Probe the SHIPPED native APEX MCP tool surface; quarantine absent/renamed endpoints (D-H22-4)
tasks.note: Pre-release prose is NOT ground truth. Introspect the real MCP's tool list on the installed H22 before any wiring. Confirmed-absent endpoints auto-quarantine without re-litigation. Gates 2.7/2.8.

tasks.title: Patch the probe deltas (promote probe truth over pinned constants)
COPs — Ocean Spectrum / texture-synth from a prompt
Solaris — scatter / set-dressing from a prompt
Wire both into the command palette (verb × context)
Provenance proof: both land in agent.usd
Karma XPU render the result on H22
Register the shipped APEX MCP as a first-class provider, truth-contract-wrapped, recorded in agent.usd (D-H22-1)
tasks.note: Swap the mock for the real surface verified by 1.7. Every MCP tool call enters the truth contract; the MCP's own validator verdict lands as a provenance INPUT, never as a Synapse claim. This is the H22 critical-path leg.

tasks.title: Scout federates the real APEX MCP; no APEX corpus (D-H22-2)
Dry-run the full runsheet, timed

human_gate.why: Human-timed; harness can't judge dead air.

tasks.title: Record the demo
Demo beat — Synapse orchestrating the native APEX MCP, with receipts
tasks.note: Prove leg. Show the boundary live: Synapse calls the native MCP as one source, the call is truth-contract-wrapped and recorded in agent.usd, and is reversible. The receipts are the one thing the native MCP can't do alone — that's the whole pitch.

tasks.title: Capsule the ship state
Utility flywheel cycle 1 — network-wiring truth (connectivity catalog -> review sweep -> wire_by_label + validator pin)
tasks.note: Cycle contract in spec-U1-wiring-flywheel.md. Anti-runaway anchors: human ratifies new cycle classes (flywheel_queue.json 'ratified'), evidence-linked queue entries only, guardrails every sprint.

tasks.title: Utility flywheel cycle U.5 — LOP/Solaris knowledge (context truth): corpus catalog -> consistency gate -> loader + additive validator advisory phase

human_gate.decision: RATIFIED 2026-07-03 (human sanction) — U.5 accepted as a context-truth cycle class; flywheel_queue.json U.5 now ratified:true

tasks.note: Cycle contract in spec-U5-lop-solaris-flywheel.md. Substantive design call: assignmaterial->material-source ordering is an ADVISORY, not a hard error (reference/sublayer composition arcs author materials too); grid/plane stay hard errors. Anti-runaway: human ratifies the new class (see human_gate).

tasks.title: v6 Session A — scaffold the skeleton: stub every module in the BP00 manifest
tasks.note: Stubs land exactly where BP00's '## Module Manifest' table says; pass bodies; zero hou imports in pure layers. The drop must be COMMITTED first — worktrees fork from HEAD, so an uncommitted docs/v6/ is invisible to this task (docs/v6/INTAKE.md checklist).

tasks.title: v6 Session B — write BP09 (Iteration Controller) at BP00–08 fidelity
tasks.note: propose -> build -> evaluate -> decide -> iterate; convergence/stop logic; max-iteration handling; pulls active strategies from BP07. Spec only — no code.

tasks.title: v6 Session C — spec + BUILD BP10 (Global Knowledge Base): pure Python, zero hou
tasks.note: Recipe store + failure DB + vector schema (spec'd; JSONL is the shipped store) + query API. The one spine piece shippable before H22. The KB public API is pre-frozen by the v6_kb_roundtrip check — BP10 documents it, it does not redesign it.

tasks.title: v6 Session D — pure-Python layers, test-first: gsplat_compare interpretation, scoring rubrics, meta-cognitive stats
tasks.note: Tests FIRST (each blueprint specifies them) so drop day is 'make them pass'.

tasks.title: v6 Miles 1–2 — BP01 Perception + BP02 G-Splat against shipped H22 (highest uncertainty first)
tasks.note: The 1.4 probe report re-ranks V.5–V.7 before work starts; build to survive 'feature X shipped differently'. probe_clean reads THIS worktree's delta artifact — re-fire the probe here first; an honest first-round FAIL until then is expected, not a bug.

tasks.title: v6 Miles 3–4 — BP08 three-tier evaluator (keystone; 01+02 become the engine)
tasks.note: Builds on V.5's output, but worktrees fork from HEAD — merge V.5 before running this, or the engine gets rebuilt divergently in-worktree. run -> merge -> re-run is the intended cadence (docs/v6/PLAN.md POST-DROP).

tasks.title: v6 Miles 5–7 — BP09+BP10 integration: first autonomous cycle, PYRO ONLY
tasks.note: Prove pyro end-to-end before branches (BP07: G-Splat convergence tracks pyro r=0.78, characters r=0.31). Branches 03–07 are production-ordered after this, not corpus-ordered. Needs V.3's KB and V.6's evaluator merged to main first — same run -> merge -> re-run cadence as V.6.

tasks.title: Context track — probe SYNAPSE's create-capability per Houdini context (SOP/LOP/COP/TOP/DOP/MAT), deposit the catalog + review sweep
tasks.note: Read-only vs product code — the probe drives the live handler surface (SynapseHandler.handle), it never edits python/synapse. The catalog is the deliverable; merging it arms C.1–C.6. Re-run per Houdini build.

tasks.title: Context track — SOP: close the top create-gap the catalog surfaces; prove by golden + ratchet
tasks.note: Read the catalog's `gaps` for sop; close the highest-leverage one by extending the HANDLER surface (new/fixed command verbs), never by weakening the probe. The golden ratchet gates: gaps must strictly decrease unless already 0. Re-run the probe in-worktree to refresh the catalog as part of the sprint. The catalog re-ranks C.1–C.6 — a context whose golden already passes with 0 gaps banks trivially, and that is correct.

tasks.title: Studio track — declare the deployment posture (mode + identity model) → write harness/state/posture.json

human_gate.decision: solo | studio | farm + identity model + auto_approve policy
human_gate.recommended: declare the mode explicitly; auto_approve only in solo; default-deny in studio/farm
human_gate.why: The report's Step-1 hinge: consent auto-approve and RBAC default-deny cannot be enforced until the deployment mode is a committed fact. Writing posture.json is the S-track trigger.

tasks.note: The S-track trigger — the fourth state-file gate, peer of drop.json. A human writes harness/state/posture.json (mode + identity_model + auto_approve); until it lands S.1–S.3 stay filtered out of the queue. Read-only: the harness surfaces the declaration template, it never authors the posture.

tasks.title: Studio track — unify the policy layer: one authoritative capability/gate/read-only/disk table checked at the transport-agnostic dispatch boundary

human_gate.decision: author the single-source policy table + wire it into registry.invoke()/handle()
human_gate.why: Security-architecture change; the harness gates + reviews it, it does not autonomously rewrite the policy layer. Closes report finding 'no single source of truth' + is the root the criticals grow from.

tasks.title: Studio track — enforce consent at the dispatch boundary: /mcp + autonomous paths get a HumanGate-wired bridge (not the disarmed panel singleton); record the real consent source in the IntegrityBlock

human_gate.decision: arm consent on the non-panel bridges + record the consent source
human_gate.why: Closes the #1 critical (consent auto-approve/absent everywhere). Security-critical; human-authored, harness-gated. The panel-path solo auto-approve stays a documented per-mode choice via posture.json.

tasks.title: Studio track — per-user identity + RBAC through dispatch on all three transports; default-deny on unresolved role/session; end the shared-handler _user_id race

human_gate.decision: move authn/RBAC to the dispatch boundary keyed by per-connection identity
human_gate.why: Closes criticals 2 + the identity race (SEC-1). Security-critical; human-authored, harness-gated.

tasks.title: Studio track — memory: one authoritative store + real provenance (source/author/tier) + recency & conflict in recall
tasks.note: loop-gradable. Closes the three memory highs (source='user' mislabel, dead tier fields, no recency → stale outranks fresh, three-store split-brain).

tasks.title: Studio track — eval backbone: wire the real validate_frame into the harness render check; guard the fake-hou sys.modules residency; pin permission boundaries as invariants
tasks.note: loop-gradable + highest-leverage-per-risk (improves the harness's OWN eval, which then better verifies every other fix). Touching harness/verify/checks.py here is deliberate self-improvement, allowed (like the P1/P2/U upgrades). The C-track goldens are the e2e demonstration this extends.

tasks.title: Studio track — farm-headless correctness: neutralize the latent PDG remove_files=True rollback; per-build the scout symbol table for mixed fleets; the TOP-headless + DOP-populate creation gaps (== C.4/C.5)
tasks.note: loop-gradable. Cross-links the C-track catalog — the TOP/DOP creation gaps live there; context_review_clean re-asserts the catalog stays sound as those gaps close.

tasks.title: Studio track — capstone studio-readiness review: aggregate every S-check, require the criticals green, emit the verdict; run the adversarial sweep
tasks.note: the "review at the end". Deterministic aggregate + verdict artifact; the run.ts adversarial Evaluator reviews it, and a human may invoke a red-team workflow (the same fan-out that produced the report) — that workflow is an OPTIONAL surface, never a deterministic dependency of this check.

tasks.title: Diagnostic track — Mile 1: cook-API confirmation probe. Every hou.* symbol on D's critical path dir()-confirmed against live hython; absences auto-quarantine. Deposits harness/notes/verified_cook_api_<build>.json
tasks.note: Phantom-first: the probe decides, not the spec's candidate list. The deposit is this check's OWN authority — NEVER spliced into h21_symbol_table.json (blake2b + dir()-membership invariant; depth-1 absences are already enforced free by phantom_clean). Precondition for every later mile.

tasks.title: Diagnostic track — Mile 2: perturbation catalog. host/introspect_cook_truth.py (hython-only, zero-synapse-import) runs perturbation trials per context (sop/lop/cop/dop) and deposits harness/notes/cook_truth_21.json — the D.3+ arming trigger
tasks.note: C.0-shaped arming task. Copy introspect_context_capability.py's discipline: artifact-write-iff-rc0, undo-wrapped + residue-verified trials, judge the FILE never stdout. May only use §1-confirmed symbols. Committing + merging the catalog arms D.3-D.5 (COOK trigger).

tasks.title: Diagnostic track — Mile 3: review sweep + per-context goldens. scripts/flywheel_review_cook.py sweeps emitter/explainer claims against the catalog; one frozen perturbation trial per context reproduces its cataloged dirty-set exactly
tasks.note: A claim the catalog can't back is a finding, not a footnote. Goldens are deterministic + render-free (Indie husk no-ops headless — renders never golden). Commandment 7: a golden that starts failing is fixed forward, never softened.

tasks.title: Diagnostic track — Mile 4: synapse_explain_recook (read-only handler on the SynapseHandler seam): given a node path (+ optional hypothetical parm change), answer what will recook and WHY from catalog + live flags, citing the trial fingerprint
tasks.note: CROWN finding #1 (ODFORCE O.1). Registered via CommandHandlerRegistry like every shipped tool — no new transport, no new panel surface. The review sweep re-runs against the explainer's claims: live truth, never doc reasoning.

tasks.title: Diagnostic track — Mile 5 (staged D.1 of the scour): synapse_diagnose_callback — replay an errored parm callback/expression under capture, return the dispatcher-style envelope (traceback_str seam) + cook-state before/after
tasks.note: CROWN finding #2 (ODFORCE O.3). Consumes Mile 1's event-callback symbols ONLY if confirmed; otherwise degrades to expression-evaluation diagnosis and says so — the truth contract: never claim capture it didn't perform.

tasks.title: Release track — mutations fail CLOSED when the integrity bridge is unavailable: remove all three direct-dispatch fallbacks (P0.2, the review's #1 code finding)
tasks.note: THREE sites (sweep found one more than the review): tool_executor + mcp/tools `except ImportError → handler.handle`, and bridge_adapter's `bridge is None → direct dispatch` (the live path). Read-only tools may degrade gracefully; mutations return a failed SynapseResponse naming the missing safety subsystem. Classification must come from an import-independent source (handlers._READ_ONLY_COMMANDS). The mcp/server.py resources lookalike is NOT a defect — leave it. Add a regression test: mutating tool errors (never dispatches) when _BRIDGE_AVAILABLE is False.

tasks.title: Release track — move the freeze heartbeat out of the panel widget into a process-lifetime beat owner (P0.3)
tasks.note: Worse than lost coverage (sweep refinement): after >=1 beat + panel close, the surviving Watchdog reads the dead beat source as a freeze and ~35s later force-opens the breaker + emergency halt on a HEALTHY session. The new owner (mark it `# RUNTIME_BEAT_SOURCE`, or def ensure_beat_started, under python/synapse/server/) must outlive panels AND handle deliberate beat-source detach. Panel demotes to displaying runtime state / calling the idempotent ensure_started.

tasks.title: Release track — gate the production sys.modules purge behind SYNAPSE_DEV_HOT_RELOAD in BOTH .pypanel loaders (P0.4)
tasks.note: Both loaders purge synapse.* unconditionally at module scope — production reopen splits singletons (duplicate bridges, stale callbacks, threads holding old class identities). Wrap in `if os.environ.get("SYNAPSE_DEV_HOT_RELOAD") == "1":` (os already imported in both) or delete outright; production reopen imports normally and reconnects to the existing runtime.

tasks.title: Release track — dependency isolation across Python minors: versioned vendor roots now, sidecar per gate-0.1's decided direction (P0.1)
tasks.note: NOT a human gate — gate-0.1 is DECIDED (sidecar, DROP_DAY.md 2026-07-10, built first post-release). The gate greens on EITHER mechanism: versioned vendor roots selected from sys.version_info (drop-window minimum; cp312/cp313 payloads pre-staged at harness/state/wheel_cache/) replacing the strict `== (3, 11)` equality, or the sidecar implementation landing under host/. Never depend on global/user site-packages. tests/test_vendored_deps.py's VENDOR_PY pin needs updating in the same commit.

tasks.title: Release track — host-targeted installer: --houdini-exe derivation + post-install host verification (P0.5)
tasks.note: Auto-detect globs only EXISTING pref dirs — a fresh H22 pref dir (created on first launch) is silently omitted; explicit $HOUDINI_USER_PREF_DIR set-but-uncreated is also dropped. Derive pref dir + hython from --houdini-exe, mkdir when absent, then verify from the host side (package parses, SYNAPSE_ROOT correct, synapse importable, pypanel + shelf discoverable). FIX_IS_REAL_PROBE: extend tests/test_install_package.py with a behavioral verify-step case — the check is presence-gated, the test is the proof. GitHub issue #3 gets answered with the verified process (human follow-up, Phase 2 of the review).

tasks.title: Release track — CI covers the shipping surface: windows-latest lane + vendored-wheel LOAD probe (P0.6)
tasks.note: The shipping platform (Windows — the only place the vendored cp311 binaries can even load) is never exercised; the wheel is asserted by FILENAME, never imported; four panel modules skip-exit-0 without PySide (the documented goalpost trap). Hosted-runner scope only: windows lane + a step that IMPORTS pydantic_core. Licensed hython/graphical lanes are receipts territory (G6) — never fake them in hosted CI.

tasks.title: Release track — shelf currency: PySide6-first clipboard + current installer message (P1, review Phase-1 item 5)
tasks.note: H21 ships PySide6 only, so the PySide2-only clipboard ALWAYS silently returns False on the target platform; the missing-panel message names the wrong installer (`python install.py` — a different, legacy mechanism). Two one-liners: PySide6-first-with-PySide2-fallback (chat_panel.py:31-36 is the repo idiom) + point at scripts/install_synapse_package.py and say 'restart Houdini'.

tasks.title: Release track — unknown-tool metadata fails closed: kill the silent set_parameter→INFORM default (P1-metadata)
tasks.note: NOT covered by S.1 (its fingerprint is shared/bridge.py's gate fallback; its taxonomy list omits bridge_adapter). An unknown tool name classifies as set_parameter → INFORM, the weakest gate — unknown capability executing with mere notification (the sibling `.get(tool_name, "HANDS")` agent default rides along). Fail closed: refuse, or classify REVIEW-or-higher; long-term the four hardcoded maps move into the S.1 single policy source.

tasks.title: Release track — fail-closed security defaults: arm the process bridge + fail-closed auth (P1-consent + P1-auth)

human_gate.decision: arm the process bridge's consent path (non-blocking approval surface) + fail-closed auth defaults
human_gate.why: Security-critical, human-authored, harness-gated (S.2/S.3 precedent). Closes the shared/bridge.py get_process_bridge disarm S.2 cannot see (`bridge._gate = None` + `consent_callback=lambda op: True`) and the entirely-ungated auth surface (no key ⇒ every token passes; empty Origin ⇒ allow; handshake skipped without a key). Under the declared solo posture these stay accepted, NAMED trade-offs; the gates keep them visible and regression-proof, and they snap to blockers under studio/farm.

tasks.note: 'User typed a prompt' is not consent for every exact tool call: keep the non-blocking UX (never HumanGate's blocking poll on the GUI thread — that deadlocks), but route delete / execute_python / disk writes / expensive renders through async approval cards. Auth: mutating endpoints require a token unless an explicit insecure-local flag is set; generate + persist ~/.synapse/auth.key on first run.

tasks.title: Release track — packaging self-containment: move shared/ into the installable package (P1, POST-stabilization by the review's own sequencing)
tasks.note: Do NOT do this inside the drop window (the review is explicit). 19 top-level `from shared...` sites under python/synapse, dual-path PYTHONPATH in BOTH package writers, plus integrity_envelope.py's import-time repo-root climb (a third site the review missed). Relocate shared/ under python/ so the wheel picks it up, rewrite imports, single-entry PYTHONPATH, update tests/test_install_package.py's dual-path pin. Non-blocking hygiene in the capstone.

tasks.title: Release track — capstone release-readiness review: aggregate every R-check + human receipts + posture into the G1–G10 map and the honest release label
tasks.note: The review's binary gates, executable. STABLE-READY only when: 7 machine gates green (P0.1–P0.6 + shelf), security legs green-or-posture-accepted, all six live receipts pass (g1 install / g5 lifecycle / g6 smoke / g7 undo / g8 restart / g9 rollback — human-authored release_receipts.json, never harness-written), host truth stamped (mode B symbol table), and G10 (README's claim never outruns the receipts). Anything less = honest 'RC — …' with blockers named. ok:true ONLY on STABLE-READY: this task is the stable-promotion gate, un-bankable until the drop is verified — by design. Verdict computable on demand regardless of the ratification hold: python harness/verify/checks.py --task R.R --worktree . --mode A

tasks.title: Knowledge track — baseline probe: snapshot corpus stats (reference-file count, lexical + semantic entry counts, retrieval mode, canonical-root check) to harness/notes/knowledge_baseline.json
tasks.note: Read-only, no hython dependency — corpus stats aren't Houdini-build-specific. Re-run scripts/knowledge_baseline.py any time to refresh; each run overwrites with a freshly-computed blake2b digest. Not gated behind anything, produces its own artifact — same shape as C.0.

tasks.title: Knowledge track — build the offline semantic (embedding) index over rag/ so scout's already-coded dense-retrieval path activates (mode lexical_only -> hybrid). Fixes: retrieval was 100% BM25/keyword matching with zero paraphrase tolerance
tasks.note: sentence-transformers is an optional extra (pyproject.toml [semantic]) — absent, scout silently stays lexical_only, no new fail-open surface. Index is built offline and committed so end users never need the embedding model installed just to GET hybrid retrieval, only to (re)build one. Known MVP limit: whole-file embedding granularity, not sub-file chunking. Acceptance: a query sharing zero literal tokens with its target doc (e.g. 'smoke sim is slow' -> pyro_fx.md) must surface that doc — verified live 2026-07-13, first hit score 1.0.

tasks.title: Knowledge track — close the Tier-1 topic-coverage gap: every reference .md file gets a semantic_index topic + reference_file pointer (was 90/117; the 27 gaps were the highest-traffic files — pyro, flip, vellum, sop_basics, rendering)
tasks.note: Data-only fix — NO change to KnowledgeIndex matching logic (a keyword-tokenization experiment was tried and reverted: it regressed precise fast-path queries like 'water sim' to gain symptom-phrasing recall that K.1's semantic path already provides — clean division of labor is Tier-1=precise keyword, K.1=paraphrase recall). 14 same-named topics relinked deterministically; 13 judgment cases decided by a 13-agent team reading each file (5 relinks to same-subject topics, 8 fresh entries with 13-16 artist-phrased keywords each). Coverage 90/117 -> 117/117, verified 2026-07-13. Root cause was topic<->file disconnection: topics like pyro_simulation existed with great keywords but never carried a reference_file to pyro_fx.md.

tasks.title: Knowledge track — retire G:\HOUDINI21_RAG_SYSTEM as scout's default RAG root (flip default to repo rag/, matching KnowledgeIndex), migrate its 803 orphaned SideFXLabs entries into rag/corpus/, archive root-level noise
tasks.note: The G: store's corpus/ entries had no searchable_text field -- a session defaulted there loaded hollow (confirmed by adversarial verification 2026-07-13). The 803 sidefxlabs_entries.json rows already matched scout's corpus schema verbatim; they were orphaned only because scout's loader scans <root>/corpus/, never <root>/semantic_index/ where they physically sat. Migrated, not regenerated. G:\HOUDINI21_RAG_SYSTEM\_raw_documentation" + garbled cp-artifact directory (embedded newline in the dirname, from a historical botched cp command) left untouched -- contains real nested sample content (FullSourceSamples, Dev_HDA_Test HDAs), not junk; needs manual handling, not a scripted move.

tasks.title: Knowledge track — the freshness gate + one-command refresh: stamp the embedding index with a content digest of its rag/ source and RED the gate when they drift. THE 'easy to update for H22' mechanism — makes doc drift loud, not silent
tasks.note: Mode A (runnable now) AND the drop-day update anchor: on the same drop.json trigger the H21->H22 tracks use, an operator authors H22 docs into rag/ then runs `python scripts/refresh_knowledge.py` (rebuild embeddings + corpus + baseline) and the gate flips green. build_semantic_index.embed_source_digest and checks.py::check_semantic_index_fresh share ONE digest formula (pure file reads, no torch) — a drift between them would break the gate. Discovered + fixed a live staleness in the same breath: K.1's vectors were built before K.2 changed semantic_index.json enrichment, so the committed embeddings were stale for ~27 relinked files (pyro_fx etc.) until this task rebuilt them. That silent staleness is exactly what the gate now prevents. Not a guardrail (would fail every sprint mid-edit); it's K.5's own verify + a K.R capstone input.

tasks.title: Knowledge track — vex-corpus -> rag/ integration: the sync ALREADY RAN and produced 14 low-quality vex_corpus_*.md files now polluting scout. DECISION: block further sync; RECOMMEND pruning the 14 (pending a human Houdini-expert call)
tasks.note: DECIDED (close-the-loop 2026-07-13, agent-investigated + spot-verified): the premise 'should we wire vex-corpus' is overtaken — vex-corpus/scripts/sync_to_synapse.py already ran and wrote 14 vex_corpus_*.md into rag/skills/houdini21-reference/ (tracked in HEAD, ~2079 chunks per .vex_corpus_manifest.json). They are live in scout (embedded 1-per-file). VERIFIED low quality: vex_corpus_point_cloud_ops.md is 5639 lines, line 1102 is broken VEX 'vector pts = pts[];', line 7 is mojibake '### Exercises a~'; ~65% of source is joy-of-vex (already cleanly in rag/ as joy_of_vex_*.md via vex_rag_pipeline) so it's largely redundant AND noisier; whole-file embedding (build_semantic_index MVP) can't represent a 5639-line file and it out-term-frequencies clean files on common VEX queries = active pollution. vex-corpus's real output is a FINE-TUNING corpus (intake.py to_training_pair), unvetted Ollama explanations, not curated retrieval. RECOMMENDATION: prune the 14 files + their semantic_index.json/agent_relevance_map.json entries, then scripts/refresh_knowledge.py (K.5 gate makes this a clean prune->red->refresh->green loop). NOT done unilaterally — pre-existing user content, a Houdini-expert quality call. Re-WIRE condition: vex-corpus grows dedup + VEX-syntax validation + section-level chunking so only net-new verified patterns remain. No H22 dependency.
